decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create powerful combos that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support the combo pieces, while also including disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure smooth gameplay and the inclusion of cards like Brightclimb Pathway and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional versatility and protection. Overall, the deck is designed to quickly assemble its combo pieces and dominate the battlefield.

The deck focuses on quickly dealing damage to the opponent using cheap spells and aggressive creatures. It aims to control the board with efficient removal and tempo plays while constantly pressuring the opponent's life total. The deck's key strategy is to maintain a fast-paced aggressive game plan while using card advantage and flexibility to adapt to different matchups. It prioritizes being proactive and maintaining pressure on the opponent while also having some element of resource denial and disruption.

This deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly in order to cast powerful spells and combo off. The combination of cards like Scapeshift, Lumra, Bellow of the Woods, and Terror of the Peaks allow for explosive plays and game-winning combos. The deck also includes cards like Underground Mortuary and Port of Karfell to help set up the combo pieces. The goal is to ramp up quickly and overwhelm the opponent with high-powered creatures and game-ending spells.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Lotus Field and Arboreal Grazer, while utilizing Vizier of Tumbling Sands to untap Lotus Field for even more mana generation. This enables the player to cast powerful spells like Omniscience and Peer into the Abyss, gaining a significant advantage in card advantage and board presence. The deck also includes control elements such as Fatal Push and Vibrance to handle opposing threats, making it a versatile and powerful combo deck in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to quickly overwhelm opponents. By utilizing cards like Fleeting Spirit, Iron-Shield Elf, and Guardian of New Benalia, this deck aims to create a strong board presence while setting up the combo. Key cards like Parhelion II and Greasefang, Okiba Boss are essential to the deck's win condition. Additionally, cards like Ral Zarek, Guest Lecturer and The Mycosynth Gardens provide support and utility to enhance the deck's overall strategy. Overall, the deck looks to efficiently assemble its combo pieces to secure victory.

The deck focuses on building a fast and aggressive board presence with low-cost creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub, supported by efficient removal options such as Skyclave Apparition and Enduring Innocence. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben can slow down opponents, while Archon of Emeria disrupts their spells. Collected Company helps find key creatures quickly, while Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides a powerful finisher. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with early aggression and high-value creatures, aiming to close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck aims to slow down the opponent's strategy and disrupt their game plan through removal spells and hand disruption. It focuses on controlling the board by using efficient removal like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push, while also utilizing cards like Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's hand. The deck also has a ramp strategy with Llanowar Elves and mana-fixing lands to ensure consistent mana production. Sheoldred, the Apocalypse serves as a win condition, providing a powerful threat that can take over the game if left unchecked.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's actions through a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a solid board presence with efficient threats like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The inclusion of cards like Thoughtseize and Abrupt Decay allows for versatile answers to a variety of threats, while cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse provide a powerful late-game win condition. The deck's mana base is carefully constructed to support its game plan and ensure consistent plays throughout the game.

This deck focuses on using Lotus Field to generate massive amounts of mana in order to quickly ramp into powerful spells and combo pieces. By sacrificing lands to Lotus Field, the player can cast high-cost spells like Scapeshift and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods early in the game. The deck also includes cards like Spelunking and Arboreal Grazer to help ramp up mana quickly. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and creatures, ultimately leading to victory.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing a combination of removal spells and hand disruption to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser to apply pressure and win the game. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and allow for consistent casting of spells. Overall, the deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy while establishing a strong board presence to secure victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells, removal, and card draw. The deck aims to generate card advantage through cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, while managing threats with Supreme Verdict and Memory Deluge. The inclusion of various lands like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Fountainport provide flexibility and utility. The deck also includes a diverse range of spells to handle different situations and maintain control over the game.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through powerful removal spells like Fatal Push, Abrupt Decay, and Witherbloom Charm. It also utilizes creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to maintain board presence and pressure the opponent. The mana base includes a mix of lands that provide both colored and colorless mana, allowing for flexibility in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while gradually building up its own resources to secure victory.

The deck focuses on leveraging the synergy between sacrificing creatures and generating value from their graveyard, with a focus on generating incremental advantage and pressuring the opponent through efficient creature removal and recursion. The deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats by utilizing cards like Witch's Oven, Scavenger's Talent, and Cauldron Familiar while disrupting the opponent's board state with cards like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push. The inclusion of cards like Ygra, Eater of All and The Meathook Massacre adds additional power and reach to the Aggro strategy.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with instants and sorceries in order to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard. The deck aims to achieve this through cards like Traumatic Critique, Consider, and Treasure Cruise while also controlling the board with cards like Fiery Impulse and Pyroclasm. Utilizing a mix of efficient spells and card draw, the deck looks to overwhelm the opponent with a consistent stream of threats and disruption. The inclusion of cards like Picklock Prankster and Cori-Steel Cutter further enhances the aggro strategy of the deck.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize the combination of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful and synergistic combo. The deck includes various support cards such as Fleeting Spirit, Guardian of New Benalia, Iron-Shield Elf, and Monument to Endurance to help protect and enhance the combo. The deck also includes removal spells like Fatal Push and hand disruption like Thoughtseize to control the game. Additionally, the mana base is carefully constructed to support the combo pieces and ensure smooth gameplay.

The deck focuses on creating a strong control board presence through a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. Utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Abrupt Decay helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Llanowar Elves and Sentinel of the Nameless City provide early mana acceleration and board presence. The inclusion of cards like Witherbloom Charm and Culling Ritual further solidify the deck's control aspect, allowing for flexibility in dealing with various threats. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of removal, disruption, and powerful creatures.
